name: MOYAMOYA DISEASE
definition: uncommon occlusive cerebral arteritis classically involving supraclinoid internal carotid underlying cause for moyamoya image identified

demographics.Age: bimodal distribution in 1st decade + 4th decade of life
demographics.Path: development of extensive tiny basal perforator and transdural collateral vessels
demographics.Histo: endothelial hyperplasia + fibrosis without associated inflammatory reaction

distribution: bilateral stenosis / occlusion of supraclinoid portion of ICA extending to proximal portions of
discriminating_features: bilateral stenosis / occlusion of supraclinoid portion of ICA extending to proximal portions of
large network of moyamoya vessels in basal ganglia + upper brainstem fed by basilar a. +
anastomoses between dural meningeal + leptomeningeal aa.
tiny flow voids commonly arising from basal cisterns extending into basal ganglia /

general: bilateral stenosis / occlusion of supraclinoid portion of ICA extending to proximal portions of
large network of moyamoya vessels in basal ganglia + upper brainstem fed by basilar a. +
anastomoses between dural meningeal + leptomeningeal aa.
MR: tiny flow voids commonly arising from basal cisterns extending into basal ganglia /
"ivy" sign = curvilinear high signal intensities along cortical sulci + brain surface = sulcal
marked leptomeningeal enhancement along cortical sulci
